tracks.meaning: the distance from the top or surface of something to the bottom
the distance from the surface of water to the bottom of a body of water
the degree of intensity, complexity, or profundity in understanding, emotion, or thought
the perpendicular distance from the surface of the skin or a body part inward
the vertical distance from the surface downward in soil, rock, or subsurface layers
the distance from front to back in a military formation or defensive position
the number of levels in a hierarchical data structure or recursion stack
the extent of a space measured from front to back
the extent of the area in front of and behind the plane of focus that appears sharp in an image
a state of being deeply involved or committed in a difficult situation
the level of detail, analysis, or resources applied to a task or problem
